skill.name: wp-interactivity-api
skill.description: Guides agents through building and debugging WordPress Interactivity API features, including directives, stores, hydration, and block view script modules.
skill.summary: Build and debug WordPress Interactivity API features with focused workflow guidance.
skill.author: Automattic

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and blocker patterns, but the cited files contain Markdown guidance and inline code spans for WordPress APIs. No executable scripts, prompt injection attempts, network exfiltration, credential access, or malicious intent were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backtick text as Ruby or shell execution. The cited lines are Markdown references to WordPress directives, package names, and examples, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown prose, inline code spans, or fenced HTML examples. There is no script runner, shell command, or user-controlled command interpolation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Has No Cryptographic Context
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at lines that contain frontmatter text, headings, or WordPress directive names. No hashing, encryption, or credential handling code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content does not mention or invoke cryptographic algorithms. The matches appear to be substring collisions in documentation text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern Is Documentation Context
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system reconnaissance, but the cited lines discuss checking browser network loading and WordPress directive behavior. No host enumeration or system data collection is requested.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are limited to WordPress debugging advice and directive descriptions. They do not instruct collection of system identity, secrets, environment data, or network topology.

content.value_statement: WordPress Interactivity API work can fail through small directive, store, or hydration mismatches. This skill gives agents a focused checklist for implementation, debugging, and verification.
actual_capabilities: Identifies Interactivity API usage through data-wp directives and @wordpress/interactivity imports.
Guides setup for block viewScriptModule integration and module build alignment.
Explains store, state, action, and callback checks for directive behavior.
Provides debugging steps for hydration issues and inert directives.
Summarizes WordPress 6.9 directive changes, including unique directive IDs.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated project scanning tools.
Requires access to the target WordPress repository for concrete fixes.
Assumes WordPress 6.9 or newer behavior for some guidance.
Does not replace official WordPress API documentation for edge cases.

use_cases.title: Debug an Inert Interactive Block
use_cases.description: Trace why directives do not fire by checking the interactive root, module loading, namespace, and browser errors.

use_cases.title: Add Interactivity to Block Markup
use_cases.description: Plan directive placement, store shape, actions, and server-rendered state before changing a block.

use_cases.title: Review a WordPress 6.9 Migration
use_cases.description: Check deprecated data-wp-ignore usage, unique directive IDs, and navigation state assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Find Interactivity API Usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner discovery in an existing repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the wp-interactivity-api skill to identify where this WordPress project uses Interactivity API directives, stores, and viewScriptModule entries.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Directive Behavior
prompt_templates.scenario: A click or submit handler does nothing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the wp-interactivity-api skill to debug why this directive does not fire. Check markup, store namespace, module loading, and hydration errors.

prompt_templates.title: Implement an Interactive Block
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a new interactive feature to a block
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the wp-interactivity-api skill to design and implement a small interactive block feature with scoped directives, state, actions, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a WordPress 6.9 Upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the wp-interactivity-api skill to review this project for WordPress 6.9 Interactivity API changes, deprecated directives, navigation state issues, and tests.

output_examples.input: A block has data-wp-on--click, but clicks do not update the UI.
output: A prioritized checklist covering module loading, interactive root placement, store namespace alignment, and console errors.
A short explanation of the most likely failure based on the repository evidence.
Verification steps for manual testing and any existing end-to-end tests.

output_examples.input: A plugin needs multiple click handlers on the same element after a WordPress update.
output: Guidance to use unique directive IDs so handlers avoid attribute collisions.
A review of affected markup and store actions.
A migration note explaining why older duplicate attribute patterns are unsafe.

output_examples.input: A theme has hydration flicker on an interactive region.
output: A comparison plan for server-rendered markup and initial client state.
Recommendations to simplify directive scope and align initial context.
A smoke test path to confirm the flicker is resolved.

best_practices: Start by locating directives, store definitions, and the module entry point before editing code.
Keep interactive roots small and map directives clearly to store state and actions.
Verify changes with a manual browser smoke test and existing end-to-end tests when available.
anti_patterns: Do not add broad interactive roots when only a small region needs state.
Do not rely on stale server state across client-side navigation.
Do not keep using deprecated data-wp-ignore behavior in WordPress 6.9 projects.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents build and debug WordPress Interactivity API features, especially directives, stores, hydration, and module loading.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains documentation and checklists. It does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which WordPress version does it target?
faq.answer: It targets WordPress 6.9 and newer behavior, while still covering general Interactivity API debugging.

faq.question: Can it create a new interactive block?
faq.answer: It can guide the workflow and recommend the official scaffold template when a new interactive block is needed.

faq.question: Can it fix hydration mismatches?
faq.answer: It can guide diagnosis and suggest changes, but it needs repository context to make concrete fixes.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
